hard_gates.requirement: Every factual slide claim resolves to the claim ledger.
Rendered slides pass browser QA before judging the deck.
A reviewer checks that each slide helps a hackathon judge score the project.

dimensions.label: Judging fit
dimensions.question: Does the deck map directly to judging criteria and time pressure?
dimensions.repair_guidance: Map every main slide to a judging criterion or move it out of the main deck.

dimensions.label: Demo clarity
dimensions.question: Can judges understand what was built and what to look for in the demo?
dimensions.repair_guidance: Replace abstract value claims with a visible demo sequence and backup proof.

dimensions.label: Evidence integrity
dimensions.question: Does the deck avoid invented traction, benchmark, model, or user claims?
dimensions.repair_guidance: Add sources, downgrade claims, or remove unsupported proof from the main story.

dimensions.label: Visual punch
dimensions.question: Does each visual make the project easier to understand quickly?
dimensions.repair_guidance: Use mechanism, before/after, demo-route, or proof visuals instead of dense copy.

dimensions.label: Delivery readiness
dimensions.question: Can the presenter deliver this inside the hackathon slot and handle questions?
dimensions.repair_guidance: Tighten the talk track, define jargon, and add backup answers for likely judge questions.

role_reviews.researcher: Check source sufficiency, hackathon criteria, and any project-specific proof gaps.
role_reviews.story_strategist: Prune to the slides judges can score inside the time limit.
role_reviews.designer: Check screenshots for speed of comprehension, hierarchy, and demo clarity.
role_reviews.critic: Review as a skeptical judge looking for proof, novelty, usability, and implementation depth.
